title: RegRipper

headings.text: Automate RegRipper in a loop (Windows CMD)
Search for Specific Keys with RECmd

overview: - r <hivefile: Registry hive file to analyze - p <plugin: Run specific plugin(s) - a: Run all plugins applicable to the hive - l: List available plugins - g: Guess hive type - f <profile: Use plugin profile (e.g., win10)

study.overview: - r <hivefile: Registry hive file to analyze - p <plugin: Run specific plugin(s) - a: Run all plugins applicable to the hive - l: List available plugins - g: Guess hive type - f <profile: Use plugin profile (e.g., win10)

keyConcepts: Automate RegRipper in a loop (Windows CMD)
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.

importantCommands.code:
rip.exe -r NTUSER.DAT -f ntuser > user.txt
rip.exe -r SYSTEM -f system > system.txt
rip.exe -r SOFTWARE -f software > software.txt
rip.exe -r SECURITY -f security > security.txt

rip.exe -r SYSTEM -p userassist
rip.exe -r NTUSER.DAT -p "shellbags,usrclass"
rip.exe -r SOFTWARE -p user*
rip.exe -r SOFTWARE -a
rip.exe -r SOFTWARE -a > software_report.txt

rip.exe -r NTUSER.DAT -p userassist > ua.txt
rip.exe -r NTUSER.DAT -p shellbags  > sb.txt
rip.exe -r NTUSER.DAT -p amcache    > am.txt
rip.exe -r NTUSER.DAT -p recentdocs > rd.txt
